bird.name: 赤颈鸊鷉
bird.summary: 赤颈鸊鷉 （学名：Podiceps grisegena），为鸊鷉科鸊鷉属的鸟类，是一种候鸟。分布于欧洲、非洲极北部海岸、亚洲西南部、西伯利亚、北美洲北部、中国等地，主要生活于低山丘陵和平原地区的各种水域中。 亚种 赤颈鸊鷉指名亚种（学名：Podiceps grisegena g…

locations.name: 西伯利亚
locations.summary: 西伯利亞（俄語：Сибирь，羅馬化：Sibir）是烏拉山脈以東廣大地區之總稱，佔北亚的大部分，面積超过1310万平方千米，東至太平洋、北至北冰洋、南至中亚北部。自16世纪下半叶以来至今属于俄罗斯，相當於該國領土总面积的75％，但仅占该国…

locations.name: 阿拉斯加州
locations.summary: 阿拉斯加州（英語：Alaska，發音：/əˈlæskə/ ；舊譯「亞尼俺國」、「亞拉士加」）是美國位於北美洲最西北端的聯邦州。州以東與加拿大的英屬哥倫比亞省和育空地區相鄰，最西端位於阿圖島，並與俄羅斯在白令海峽以西有一海上邊界。以北有楚科…

locations.name: 明尼蘇達州
locations.summary: 明尼蘇達州（State of Minnesota，/ˌmɪnəˈsoʊtə/ MIN-ə-SOH-tə）是美國第32個州，於1858年5月11日加入聯邦。該州位於美國中西部的上中西部地區，北接加拿大曼尼托巴省與安大略省，東鄰威斯康辛州，南…

locations.name: 堪察加半島
locations.summary: 堪察加半島（俄語：Полуостров Камчатка，羅馬化：Poluostrov Kamchatka），清朝稱甘查甲、岡扎德加，是位于东北亚的一个半岛，由俄羅斯遠東聯邦管區堪察加邊疆區管辖。堪察加半岛長1,250公里，面积37万平方…

locations.name: 北海道
locations.summary: 北海道（日語：北海道／ほっかいどう〔ほくかいだう〕，羅馬化：Hokkaidō，）是日本最北的一級行政區，亦是日本现时唯一以「道」為名的行政区划，首府為札幌市。北海道面積共83,423.84平方公里（包括仍有爭議的南千島群島在內），是日本面…

locations.name: 不列颠哥伦比亚省
locations.summary: British Columbia is the westernmost province of Canada.

locations.name: 加利福尼亚州
locations.summary: 加利福尼亚州（英語：State of California），簡稱加州，是美国西部太平洋沿岸的一个州。面積列美國第三；人口為3,918萬，列美國各州第一。州首府是萨克拉门托。在地理、地貌、物產、人口構成方面都具有多样化的特點。加州有一别名…

locations.name: 紐芬蘭與拉布拉多省
locations.summary: 紐芬蘭與拉布拉多省（英語：Newfoundland and Labrador，法語：Terre-Neuve-et-Labrador，舊稱紐芬蘭省），簡稱紐省，是加拿大的十個省之一。紐芬蘭與拉布拉多省由兩部分組成：位於北美大陸以東的纽芬兰岛…

locations.name: 佛罗里达州
locations.summary: 佛罗里达州（英語：State of Florida），简称佛州，是位于美国东南部的一个州。该州西临墨西哥湾，西北与阿拉巴马州接壤，北邻佐治亚州，东濒大西洋，南接佛罗里达海峡，并与古巴隔海相望。佛罗里达州大部分土地位于墨西哥湾和大西洋之间的…

relations.subject: 赤颈鸊鷉
relations.evidence: holbolii (sometimes called Holbøll's grebe), found in North America and eastern Siberia, is evaluated as Least Concern, and the global population is stable or growing

relations.subject: 赤颈鸊鷉
relations.evidence: holbolii breeds in North America in Alaska, western and central Canada, and the northern US east to Minnesota; in Asia it nests in eastern Siberia from Kamchatka south to Hokkaido and west to Mongolia

relations.subject: 赤颈鸊鷉
relations.evidence: The Asian birds winter at sea from Japan to the East China Sea, and American breeders winter in the Pacific, mainly from southern Alaska to British Columbia (with smaller numbers south to California), and in the Atlantic from Newfoundland and Labrador to Florida

relations.subject: 赤颈鸊鷉
relations.evidence: The red-necked grebe shows a preference for waters in forested areas or, further north, in shrub tundra, and favours sites with abundant emergent vegetation, such as reedbeds. The best breeding habitat is fish-ponds, which have an abundance of food in addition to meeting the other requirements. The American subspecies is less tied to a high aquatic plant density, and sometimes breeds on quite open lakes. All populations are migratory and winter mainly at sea, usually in estuaries and bays, but often well offshore where fish are within diving reach near shallow banks or islands. The preferred passage and wintering habitat is water less than deep with a sand or gravel bottom, scattered rocks and patches of seaweed. During winter, birds typically feed alone and rarely aggregate into flocks, but on migration, concentrations of over 2000 individuals may occur at favoured staging sites. Migration is usually at night, but may occur during the day, especially when over water
relations.object_summary: 海湾、泻湖、盐沼与河口组成的复合湿地。

relations.subject: 赤颈鸊鷉
relations.evidence: The red-necked grebe shows a preference for waters in forested areas or, further north, in shrub tundra, and favours sites with abundant emergent vegetation, such as reedbeds. The best breeding habitat is fish-ponds, which have an abundance of food in addition to meeting the other requirements. The American subspecies is less tied to a high aquatic plant density, and sometimes breeds on quite open lakes. All populations are migratory and winter mainly at sea, usually in estuaries and bays, but often well offshore where fish are within diving reach near shallow banks or islands. The preferred passage and wintering habitat is water less than deep with a sand or gravel bottom, scattered rocks and patches of seaweed. During winter, birds typically feed alone and rarely aggregate into flocks, but on migration, concentrations of over 2000 individuals may occur at favoured staging sites. Migration is usually at night, but may occur during the day, especially when over water
relations.object_summary: 淡水与海水交汇形成的高生产力边缘生态系统。

relations.subject: 赤颈鸊鷉
relations.evidence: In: Proceedings of the 4th Meeting of the ICAZ Bird Working Group Kraków, Poland, 11–15 September 2001|journal=Acta Zoologica Cracoviensia|volume=45|pages=109–116|url=http://www.isez.pan.krakow.pl/journals/azc_v/pdf/45/08.pdf|access-date=31 August 2008|archive-date=4 March 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304114318/http://www.isez.pan.krakow.pl/journals/azc_v/pdf/45/08.pdf}} but there is no evidence that there is any significant level of hunting at the present time. In North America, there are potential threats from pollutants such as polychlorinated biphenyls (PCBs) and pesticides such as DDT which cause reduced reproductive success due to egg sterility and eggshell thinning
relations.object_summary: 农业化学品通过水体和食物链影响鸟类生存。

relations.object_summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

relations.subject: 赤颈鸊鷉
relations.evidence: Breeding areas may be threatened by the modification and degradation of lakes and by human disturbance from water-based recreational activities. There is no evidence to suggest that these threats could result in a significant risk to the overall population; more than 70% of North American red-necked grebes breed in Canada, where the population is stable or increasing
relations.object_summary: 旅游、施工和高频活动影响繁殖或觅食。
